description: Context-aware eval — questions with [Patient context: ...] prefix and cancerType filter, simulating real UI usage.

failures.issues: Thin HR+ breast evidence. Missing molecular relapse term, median lead time, Parsons et al. c-TRAK TN cross-subtype extrapolation not adequately flagged.
Sparse HNSCC DB content — only 1/8 sources head_neck-specific. HPV ctDNA as distinct analyte not mentioned. Cross-indication broadening defeated the cancer type filter.

systemicIssues: Cancer type filter significantly improves CRC questions (clean sources, no contamination)
Filter helps non-CRC when DB has adequate content (breast 8→9, bladder 8→10)
Filter cannot help with sparse DB content — HNSCC, pancreatic, melanoma still get cross-indication sources via broadening
RUBY trial (endometrial) still leaks through NSCLC filter — contamination gate issue
System handles sparse-evidence cancer types gracefully — honest about limitations, labels cross-indication data

improvements: Q104 TNBC: 8→9 (breast filter eliminated CRC contamination)
Q106 NSCLC: 8→9 (RUBY not cited in body despite appearing in sources)
Q110 Bladder: 8→10 (IMvigor011 PMID correctly cited, bladder-specific sources)
regressions: Q108 HNSCC: 8→7 (fewer on-topic sources retrieved with filter)
unchanged: Q101/Q102 CRC: 10/10 (CRC already strong without filter)
Q105 HR+ breast: 7/7 (persistent thin evidence for this subtype)

results.summary: Excellent CRC escalation response. GALAXY, IDEA, CIRCULATE-US, CLAUDIA all cited. Clean CRC-only sources. No cross-indication contamination.
Strong de-escalation response. IDEA HR 1.16, GALAXY, CIRCULATE-US cited. CAPOX/FOLFOX distinction correct. CRC-specific sources only.
Improved over non-filtered Q4. All 8 sources breast-specific — no CRC contamination. c-TRAK TN and Artemis correctly described. Missing olaparib/BRCA. PERSEVERE in references.

FAIL: Assay misattribution resolved (no Signatera/RaDaR confusion), but evidence thin. Missing molecular relapse term, median lead time, Parsons et al. c-TRAK TN cross-subtype extrapolation inadequately flagged. TREAT ctDNA trial correctly surfaced for HR+.

ADAURA correctly described. MERMAID-1 EGFR+ exclusion noted. RUBY trial (endometrial) still in sources [6] but not cited in body — contamination gate issue persists but doesn't affect response quality.

results.context: Head and Neck, Stage II, Pre-treatment
results.summary: FAIL: HPV ctDNA as distinct analyte not mentioned. Only 1/8 sources HNSCC-specific. Sparse DB content — filter triggered broadening that filled sources with CRC/gastric/bladder. NavDx correctly identified.
Major improvement over non-filtered Q10 (8→10). IMvigor011 PMID 41124204 cited correctly. First positive ctDNA-guided immunotherapy RCT described accurately. Bladder-specific sources prioritized.
Graceful handling of sparse-evidence cancer type. AMPLIFY-201 (KRAS) correctly cited. Cross-indication CRC data clearly labeled. Honest about pancreatic-specific gaps.
Outstanding nuanced response. Both sides of MSI-H + MRD- interaction presented with clinical sophistication. GALAXY, ASCO, NCCN all correctly cited. Evidence gaps precisely identified.
Honest about minimal melanoma ctDNA data. NCT05060003 (7 patients) correctly cited. COMBI-AD standard 12-month duration referenced. Cross-indication data clearly labeled.
